steps.title: Calculate Jupiter's weight using the given percentage
steps.content:
Jupiter's weight is 252.8% of Earth's weight:

$$\text{Weight on Jupiter} = 2.528 \times 220.00 = 556.16 \text{ pounds}$$

steps.title: Find x where Saturn weight equals x% of Jupiter weight
steps.content:
Set up the proportion where Saturn's weight is $x$% of Jupiter's weight:

$$234.52 = \frac{x}{100} \times 556.16$$

Solve for $x$:

$$x = \frac{234.52}{556.16} \times 100 \approx 42.17$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
